meta.name: French Country Roast Chicken
meta.subtitle: Herb-butter (or olive oil) roast with pan jus; crisp skin, juicy meat.

source.name: ssa.cuisines.french_country
notes: Canonical SSA recipe: stable ids, deterministic steps, adapter-friendly timers and checks.
Designed for standard home ovens; optional cast-iron or roasting pan workflows included.

summary: A classic French country roast chicken: dry-brined for flavor, roasted hot for crisp skin, then finished with a quick pan jus. Great for leftovers (salads, sandwiches, soup).

yield.notes: 1 whole chicken serves 4 (or 2 with generous leftovers).

notes: Active time is mostly prep + basting/monitoring.
Hands-off time depends on bird size and oven accuracy.
Herb-forward, savory, classic pan jus.
Optional lemon adds brightness (still low acid overall).

optional.label: Dutch oven / cocotte
optional.notes: Great for self-basting; produces rich drippings.

optional.label: Cast-iron skillet
optional.notes: Nice for smaller birds; encourages browning.

ingredients.label: Whole chicken
notes: 3.5–4.5 lb / 1.6–2.0 kg works well.
If larger than ~5 lb, extend roast time; rely on internal temp.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: About 1.5–2 tsp Diamond Crystal; adjust if using fine salt.
This amount is for dry-brine on a ~4 lb bird.

ingredients.label: Unsalted butter, softened
notes: Optional substitution: use olive oil for dairy-free (see variations).

ingredients.label: Olive oil
notes: Use with butter for spreadability, or replace butter entirely for dairy-free.

ingredients.label: Garlic
ingredients.notes: 2 cloves minced for herb butter; 2 cloves left whole for cavity (optional).

ingredients.label: Fresh thyme
ingredients.notes: Or 1 tsp dried thyme if needed.

ingredients.label: Fresh rosemary
notes: Chopped (or 1 tsp dried). Optional but classic.

ingredients.label: Fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: Chopped; optional garnish and for herb butter.

ingredients.label: Lemon
ingredients.notes: Halved; for cavity + finishing (optional but recommended).

ingredients.label: Yellow onion
ingredients.notes: Quartered; forms a flavorful roasting bed.

ingredients.label: Carrots
ingredients.notes: Rough chunks; optional but helps pan drippings.

ingredients.label: Celery stalks
ingredients.notes: Rough chunks; optional but helps pan drippings.

ingredients.label: Dry white wine
ingredients.notes: For pan jus. Substitute stock if preferred.

ingredients.label: Chicken stock
ingredients.notes: For pan jus (or use instead of wine).

steps.title: Dry-brine (best) or season right before roasting
instructions: Pat the chicken very dry with paper towels (inside and out).
Season all over with kosher salt and black pepper, including the cavity.
For best results: place uncovered on a rack over a tray and refrigerate 8–24 hours to dry-brine (crisper skin, deeper seasoning).
If short on time: proceed immediately—just be extra thorough with drying the skin.

steps.title: Preheat oven and set up the roasting pan
instructions: Heat oven to 425°F (220°C).
Add onion (and optional carrot/celery) to the roasting pan to form a bed.
If using a rack: set it over the vegetables. If not, place the chicken directly on the vegetables.

temperature.notes: High heat helps crisp skin; you may lower later depending on browning.

steps.title: Make herb butter (or oil rub)
instructions: In a small bowl, mash softened butter with olive oil, minced garlic, chopped thyme leaves, and optional rosemary/parsley.
If dairy-free: use only olive oil and herbs/garlic (skip butter).
checks: Garlic is minced fine so it won’t burn on the skin.

steps.title: Truss and flavor the cavity
instructions: Optional but helpful: tuck wing tips behind the back.
Stuff cavity with 2 whole garlic cloves, thyme sprigs, and optional lemon halves.
Tie legs loosely with kitchen twine (or simply cross legs).
Rub herb butter (or oil) all over the skin. If you can, gently loosen skin over the breast and smear a bit under the skin for extra flavor.
checks: Wings are tucked so they won’t burn.
Rub covers breast, thighs, and legs evenly.

steps.title: Roast until golden and cooked through
instructions: Place chicken breast-side up on rack/vegetables.
Roast at 425°F (220°C) for 20 minutes to set the skin.
Reduce oven to 375°F (190°C) and continue roasting until done.
Start checking temperature at 45 minutes total cook time for smaller birds; most 4 lb birds take ~60–75 minutes total.
Chicken is done when thickest part of breast reaches 160–165°F (71–74°C) and thigh reaches 170–175°F (77–79°C).
checks: Juices run mostly clear at the thigh joint.

steps.title: Rest the chicken (don’t skip)
instructions: Transfer chicken to a cutting board.
Tent loosely with foil and rest 10–20 minutes before carving.
Resting finishes carryover cooking and keeps juices in the meat.
checks: Rested at least 10 minutes before slicing breast.

steps.title: Make quick pan jus (optional but recommended)
instructions: Pour off excess fat from the roasting pan, leaving browned bits and 1–2 tbsp fat behind.
Place pan over medium heat (or scrape everything into a saucepan).
Deglaze with wine (if using) and reduce by about half, scraping up browned bits.
Add chicken stock and simmer 3–5 minutes until slightly reduced.
Taste and adjust: add a squeeze of lemon (optional), a pinch of salt, and pepper.
Optional: whisk in a small knob of butter off heat for shine (skip if dairy-free).
Strain if you want a smoother jus.
checks: Jus tastes savory and balanced (not overly salty).
No burnt bits—scrape only the browned fond, not black scorch.

steps.title: Carve and serve
instructions: Remove twine and discard cavity aromatics.
Carve legs/thighs first, then slice breast against the grain.
Serve with pan jus and a simple side (roasted vegetables, salad, potatoes, or bread).
checks: Breast sliced across grain for tenderness.

notes: Classic: roast chicken + simple salad with vinaigrette.
Also great with lentils or a rustic vegetable soup.
Cool leftovers promptly (within 2 hours).
Store meat in an airtight container; keep jus separate if possible.
Freeze picked meat in portions for soup, pot pie, or quick meals.
Freeze carcass for stock (best within 1–2 days).
Best: rewarm gently in a covered pan with a splash of stock/jus.
Avoid overheating breast meat; aim for just-hot.

core: Avoid cross-contamination: separate board/knife or wash thoroughly after raw poultry.
Chill leftovers quickly and reheat to steaming hot.
allergenNotes: Contains dairy if butter is used. Use olive oil to make dairy-free.

notes: If skin browns too fast: tent breast loosely with foil for the last 15–20 minutes.
If you want extra-crisp skin: air-dry uncovered overnight (dry-brine) and keep the chicken elevated on a rack.
Optional add-on: scatter small potatoes around the pan; toss in drippings mid-roast.

replaceIngredients.notes: Use 2–3 tbsp olive oil plus herbs/garlic; finish jus without butter.

notes: Preheat dutch oven 10 minutes, add aromatics, place chicken inside uncovered.
Roast uncovered for browning; cover loosely if drippings risk burning.
Use 1–2 tsp herbes de Provence in place of separate herbs.

remixIdeas: Sandwiches on crusty bread with pan jus
